segments.text: I have a question. How did bullet train go from sounding like explosions to whisper quiet? Each design breakthrough made them faster and quicker. But the final one is going to blow your mind.
Japan's bullet train hit 170 mph, but created deafening sonic booms when exiting tunnels. Even 400 m away, it was
loud as a gunshot. So engineers tried hundreds of nose designs. Sharper points, different angles, smooth surfaces, nothing worked. The train
stayed loud. Then one engineer who loved bird watching had an idea. He watched a kingfisher dive into water at high speeds with no splash. The secret was
its long pointed beak that cut through the water like a knife. So engineers copied three birds completely. Owl feathers for silent electrical
connections, penguin body for smooth air flow, and the king fisher beak for the nose. And the final result, train that is faster, quieter, and more efficient.
It turns out nature is the best designer. Subscribe for more.
